Jones.All rights reserved.Last revised 5 May 2025.Rendezvous and DockingThe Ascent burn lasted about 7 minutes and 18 seconds and it put the Challenger crew into an elliptical orbit with a low point of 9 nautical miles above the Moon and a high point of 50.
It was a nearly perfect burn.
At 12 minutes 12 seconds after launch, Gene did series of RCS (Reaction Control System) firings - called "tweak burns" - to fine tune the orbit.

Compared with their 5500 foot-per-second (fps) orbital speed, the velocity change that Gene wanted - 4 fps aft (down through the ascent engine bell), 1 fps out theplus-Zaxis (out the windows), and 9 fps left (toward Gene's side of the spacecraft) - was trivial in the extreme.
However, they wouldn't rendezvous with Ron for another hour and a half - not until they'd gone around the backside of the Moon and were in radio contact w...
